package repository

import (
	"fmt"

	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for db_query_argument_erased_to_any.
// Rule intent: Concrete DB arguments should not be erased to `any` or `interface{}`
// immediately before query execution, as this loses type safety.
// Anti-pattern: Explicitly casting typed values to `any` before passing to query methods.

type UserRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

func (r *UserRepository) FindByTenantID(tenantID string) ([]User, error) {
	var users []User

	// Anti-pattern: typed string erased to any before query
	var arg any = tenantID
	r.db.Where("tenant_id = ?", arg).Find(&users)

	return users, nil
}

func (r *UserRepository) FindByStatusAndRole(status string, role string) ([]User, error) {
	var users []User

	// Anti-pattern: multiple typed values erased to any
	var statusArg any = status
	var roleArg any = role
	r.db.Where("status = ? AND role = ?", statusArg, roleArg).Find(&users)

	return users, nil
}

func (r *UserRepository) FindByID(id int64) (*User, error) {
	var user User

	// Anti-pattern: int64 erased to any before query
	var idArg any = id
	r.db.Where("id = ?", idArg).First(&user)

	if user.ID == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("user not found")
	}

	return &user, nil
}

type User struct {
	ID       int64  `gorm:"column:id"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id"`
	Status   string `gorm:"column:status"`
	Role     string `gorm:"column:role"`
	Name     string `gorm:"column:name"`
}
